mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real personal consumption (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with sustained RSAFS growth (rolling 3-month +1% threshold).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, leading to positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate inflationary pressures forcing rate hikes, creating negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO signals reduced industrial output, creating deflationary risks that drive SPX negative returns during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of INDPRO decline periods during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDPC1 and HOUST indicates robust demand for goods and housing, driving SPX positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A 100k monthly decline in PAYEMS signals weakening labor markets, reducing consumer spending and causing SPX negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of PAYEMS decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 20bp increase in FEDFUNDS after a 3-month average below 4% signals aggressive tightening, creating negative SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of FEDFUNDS spike instances post-low-rate environment.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with declining GDPC1 indicates supply-side bottlenecks disrupting production, causing SPX negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 40% of periods with conflicting INDPRO/GDPC1 signals.
